ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلطُّورِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waalttoori
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: By the mount
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: By Mount Ṭûr!

ayahs.textArabic: وَكِتَٰبٖ مَّسۡطُورٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakitabin mastoorin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [by] a Book inscribed
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And by the Book written

ayahs.textArabic: فِي رَقّٖ مَّنشُورٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fee raqqin manshoorin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In parchment spread open
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: on open pages ˹for all to read˺!

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلۡبَيۡتِ ٱلۡمَعۡمُورِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waalbayti almaAAmoori
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [by] the frequented House
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And by the ˹Sacred˺ House frequently visited!

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلسَّقۡفِ ٱلۡمَرۡفُوعِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waalssaqfi almarfooAAi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [by] the heaven raised high
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And by the canopy raised ˹high˺!

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلۡبَحۡرِ ٱلۡمَسۡجُورِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waalbahri almasjoori
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [by] the sea filled [with fire],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And by the seas set on fire!

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ عَذَابَ رَبِّكَ لَوَٰقِعٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna AAathaba rabbika lawaqiAAun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, the punishment of your Lord will occur.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, the punishment of your Lord will come to pass—

ayahs.textArabic: مَّا لَهُۥ مِن دَافِعٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ma lahu min dafiAAin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Of it there is no preventer.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: none will avert it—

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَ تَمُورُ ٱلسَّمَآءُ مَوۡرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawma tamooru alssamao mawran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: On the Day the heaven will sway with circular motion
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: on the Day the heavens will be shaken violently,

ayahs.textArabic: وَتَسِيرُ ٱلۡجِبَالُ سَيۡرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wataseeru aljibalu sayran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the mountains will pass on, departing -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the mountains will be blown away entirely.

ayahs.textArabic: فَوَيۡلٞ يَوۡمَئِذٖ لِّلۡمُكَذِّبِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fawaylun yawmaithin lilmukaththibeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then woe, that Day, to the deniers,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then woe on that Day to the deniers—

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ هُمۡ فِي خَوۡضٖ يَلۡعَبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allatheena hum fee khawdin yalAAaboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Who are in [empty] discourse amusing themselves.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: those who amuse themselves with falsehood!

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَ يُدَعُّونَ إِلَىٰ نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ دَعًّا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawma yudaAAAAoona ila nari jahannama daAAAAan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Day they are thrust toward the fire of Hell with a [violent] thrust, [its angels will say],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹It is˺ the Day they will be fiercely shoved into the Fire of Hell.

ayahs.textArabic: هَٰذِهِ ٱلنَّارُ ٱلَّتِي كُنتُم بِهَا تُكَذِّبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hathihi alnnaru allatee kuntum biha tukaththiboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: "This is the Fire which you used to deny.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They will be told,˺ “This is the Fire which you used to deny.

ayahs.textArabic: أَفَسِحۡرٌ هَٰذَآ أَمۡ أَنتُمۡ لَا تُبۡصِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Afasihrun hatha am antum la tubsiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then is this magic, or do you not see?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Is this magic, or do you not see?

ayahs.textArabic: ٱصۡلَوۡهَا فَٱصۡبِرُوٓاْ أَوۡ لَا تَصۡبِرُواْ سَوَآءٌ عَلَيۡكُمۡۖ إِنَّمَا تُجۡزَوۡنَ مَا كُنتُمۡ تَعۡمَلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Islawha faisbiroo aw la tasbiroo sawaon AAalaykum innama tujzawna ma kuntum taAAmaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Enter to] burn therein; then be patient or impatient - it is all the same for you. You are only being recompensed [for] what you used to do."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Burn in it! It is the same whether you endure ˹it˺ patiently or not. You are only rewarded for what you used to do.”

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ ٱلۡمُتَّقِينَ فِي جَنَّٰتٖ وَنَعِيمٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna almuttaqeena fee jannatin wanaAAeemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, the righteous will be in gardens and pleasure,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, the righteous will be in Gardens and bliss,

ayahs.textArabic: فَٰكِهِينَ بِمَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمۡ رَبُّهُمۡ وَوَقَىٰهُمۡ رَبُّهُمۡ عَذَابَ ٱلۡجَحِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fakiheena bima atahum rabbuhum wawaqahum rabbuhum AAathaba aljaheemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Enjoying what their Lord has given them, and their Lord protected them from the punishment of Hellfire.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: enjoying whatever their Lord will have granted them. And their Lord will have protected them from the torment of the Hellfire.

ayahs.textArabic: كُلُواْ وَٱشۡرَبُواْ هَنِيٓـَٔۢا بِمَا كُنتُمۡ تَعۡمَلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kuloo waishraboo haneean bima kuntum taAAmaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [They will be told], "Eat and drink in satisfaction for what you used to do."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They will be told,˺ “Eat and drink happily for what you used to do.”

ayahs.textArabic: مُتَّكِـِٔينَ عَلَىٰ سُرُرٖ مَّصۡفُوفَةٖۖ وَزَوَّجۡنَٰهُم بِحُورٍ عِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Muttakieena AAala sururin masfoofatin wazawwajnahum bihoorin AAeenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will be reclining on thrones lined up, and We will marry them to fair women with large, [beautiful] eyes.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will be reclining on thrones, ˹neatly˺ lined up ˹facing each other˺. And We will pair them to maidens with gorgeous eyes.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَٱتَّبَعَتۡهُمۡ ذُرِّيَّتُهُم بِإِيمَٰنٍ أَلۡحَقۡنَا بِهِمۡ ذُرِّيَّتَهُمۡ وَمَآ أَلَتۡنَٰهُم مِّنۡ عَمَلِهِم مِّن شَيۡءٖۚ كُلُّ ٱمۡرِيِٕۭ بِمَا كَسَبَ رَهِينٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena amanoo waittabaAAathum thurriyyatuhum bieemanin alhaqna bihim thurriyyatahum wama alatnahum min AAamalihim min shayin kullu imriin bima kasaba raheenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who believed and whose descendants followed them in faith - We will join with them their descendants, and We will not deprive them of anything of their deeds. Every person, for what he earned, is retained.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: As for those who believe and whose descendants follow them in faith, We will elevate their descendants to their rank, never discounting anything ˹of the reward˺ of their deeds. Every person will reap only what they sowed.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَمۡدَدۡنَٰهُم بِفَٰكِهَةٖ وَلَحۡمٖ مِّمَّا يَشۡتَهُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waamdadnahum bifakihatin walahmin mimma yashtahoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We will provide them with fruit and meat from whatever they desire.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We will ˹continually˺ provide them with whatever fruit or meat they desire.

ayahs.textArabic: يَتَنَٰزَعُونَ فِيهَا كَأۡسٗا لَّا لَغۡوٞ فِيهَا وَلَا تَأۡثِيمٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: YatanazaAAoona feeha kasan la laghwun feeha wala tatheemun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will exchange with one another a cup [of wine] wherein [results] no ill speech or commission of sin.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will pass around to each other a drink ˹of pure wine,˺ which leads to no idle talk or sinfulness.

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ وَيَطُوفُ عَلَيۡهِمۡ غِلۡمَانٞ لَّهُمۡ كَأَنَّهُمۡ لُؤۡلُؤٞ مَّكۡنُونٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wayatoofu AAalayhim ghilmanun lahum kaannahum luluon maknoonun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: There will circulate among them [servant] boys [especially] for them, as if they were pearls well-protected.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And they will be waited on by their youthful servants like spotless pearls.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَقۡبَلَ بَعۡضُهُمۡ عَلَىٰ بَعۡضٖ يَتَسَآءَلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waaqbala baAAduhum AAala baAAdin yatasaaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they will approach one another, inquiring of each other.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will turn to one another inquisitively.

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُوٓاْ إِنَّا كُنَّا قَبۡلُ فِيٓ أَهۡلِنَا مُشۡفِقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo inna kunna qablu fee ahlina mushfiqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will say, "Indeed, we were previously among our people fearful [of displeasing Allah].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will say, “Before ˹this reward˺ we used to be in awe ˹of Allah˺ in the midst of our people.

ayahs.textArabic: فَمَنَّ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيۡنَا وَوَقَىٰنَا عَذَابَ ٱلسَّمُومِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Famanna Allahu AAalayna wawaqana AAathaba alssamoomi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So Allah conferred favor upon us and protected us from the punishment of the Scorching Fire.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So Allah has graced us and protected us from the torment of ˹Hell’s˺ scorching heat.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّا كُنَّا مِن قَبۡلُ نَدۡعُوهُۖ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلۡبَرُّ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna kunna min qablu nadAAoohu innahu huwa albarru alrraheemu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, we used to supplicate Him before. Indeed, it is He who is the Beneficent, the Merciful."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, we used to call upon Him ˹alone˺ before. He is truly the Most Kind, Most Merciful.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَذَكِّرۡ فَمَآ أَنتَ بِنِعۡمَتِ رَبِّكَ بِكَاهِنٖ وَلَا مَجۡنُونٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fathakkir fama anta biniAAmati rabbika bikahin wala majnoonin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So remind [O Muhammad], for you are not, by the favor of your Lord, a soothsayer or a madman.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So ˹continue to˺ remind ˹all, O Prophet˺. For you, by the grace of your Lord, are not a fortune-teller or a madman.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ يَقُولُونَ شَاعِرٞ نَّتَرَبَّصُ بِهِۦ رَيۡبَ ٱلۡمَنُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am yaqooloona shaAAirun natarabbasu bihi rayba almanooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do they say [of you], "A poet for whom we await a misfortune of time?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do they say, “˹He is˺ a poet, for whom we ˹eagerly˺ await an ill-fate!”?

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ تَرَبَّصُواْ فَإِنِّي مَعَكُم مِّنَ ٱلۡمُتَرَبِّصِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul tarabbasoo fainnee maAAakum mina almutarabbiseena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "Wait, for indeed I am, with you, among the waiters."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, “Keep waiting! I too am waiting with you.”

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ تَأۡمُرُهُمۡ أَحۡلَٰمُهُم بِهَٰذَآۚ أَمۡ هُمۡ قَوۡمٞ طَاغُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am tamuruhum ahlamuhum bihatha am hum qawmun taghoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do their minds command them to [say] this, or are they a transgressing people?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do their ˹intelligent˺ minds prompt them to this ˹paradox˺? Or are they ˹just˺ a transgressing people?

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ يَقُولُونَ تَقَوَّلَهُۥۚ بَل لَّا يُؤۡمِنُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am yaqooloona taqawwalahu bal la yuminoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do they say, "He has made it up"? Rather, they do not believe.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do they say, “He made this ˹Quran˺ up!”? In fact, they have no faith.

ayahs.textArabic: فَلۡيَأۡتُواْ بِحَدِيثٖ مِّثۡلِهِۦٓ إِن كَانُواْ صَٰدِقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Falyatoo bihadeethin mithlihi in kanoo sadiqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then let them produce a statement like it, if they should be truthful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Let them then produce something like it, if what they say is true!

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ خُلِقُواْ مِنۡ غَيۡرِ شَيۡءٍ أَمۡ هُمُ ٱلۡخَٰلِقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am khuliqoo min ghayri shayin am humu alkhaliqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or were they created by nothing, or were they the creators [of themselves]?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or were they created by nothing, or are they ˹their own˺ creators?

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ خَلَقُواْ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَۚ بَل لَّا يُوقِنُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am khalaqoo alssamawati waalarda bal la yooqinoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or did they create the heavens and the earth? Rather, they are not certain.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or did they create the heavens and the earth? In fact, they have no firm belief ˹in Allah˺.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ عِندَهُمۡ خَزَآئِنُ رَبِّكَ أَمۡ هُمُ ٱلۡمُصَۜيۡطِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am AAindahum khazainu rabbika am humu almusaytiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or have they the depositories [containing the provision] of your Lord? Or are they the controllers [of them]?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do they possess the treasuries of your Lord, or are they in control ˹of everything˺?

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ لَهُمۡ سُلَّمٞ يَسۡتَمِعُونَ فِيهِۖ فَلۡيَأۡتِ مُسۡتَمِعُهُم بِسُلۡطَٰنٖ مُّبِينٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am lahum sullamun yastamiAAoona feehi falyati mustamiAAuhum bisultanin mubeenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or have they a stairway [into the heaven] upon which they listen? Then let their listener produce a clear authority.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do they have a stairway, by which they eavesdrop ˹on the heavens˺? Then let those who do so bring a compelling proof.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ لَهُ ٱلۡبَنَٰتُ وَلَكُمُ ٱلۡبَنُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am lahu albanatu walakumu albanoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or has He daughters while you have sons?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or does He have daughters ˹as you claim˺, while you ˹prefer to˺ have sons?

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ تَسۡـَٔلُهُمۡ أَجۡرٗا فَهُم مِّن مَّغۡرَمٖ مُّثۡقَلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am tasaluhum ajran fahum min maghramin muthqaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do you, [O Muhammad], ask of them a payment, so they are by debt burdened down?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or are you ˹O Prophet˺ asking them for a reward ˹for the message˺ so that they are overburdened by debt?

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ عِندَهُمُ ٱلۡغَيۡبُ فَهُمۡ يَكۡتُبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am AAindahumu alghaybu fahum yaktuboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or have they [knowledge of] the unseen, so they write [it] down?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do they have access to ˹the Record in˺ the unseen, so they copy it ˹for all to see˺?

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ يُرِيدُونَ كَيۡدٗاۖ فَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ هُمُ ٱلۡمَكِيدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am yureedoona kaydan faallatheena kafaroo humu almakeedoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do they intend a plan? But those who disbelieve - they are the object of a plan.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do they intend to scheme ˹against the Prophet˺? Then it is the disbelievers who will fall victim to ˹their˺ schemes.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ لَهُمۡ إِلَٰهٌ غَيۡرُ ٱللَّهِۚ سُبۡحَٰنَ ٱللَّهِ عَمَّا يُشۡرِكُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am lahum ilahun ghayru Allahi subhana Allahi AAamma yushrikoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or have they a deity other than Allah? Exalted is Allah above whatever they associate with Him.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do they have a god other than Allah? Glorified is Allah far above what they associate ˹with Him˺!

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِن يَرَوۡاْ كِسۡفٗا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ سَاقِطٗا يَقُولُواْ سَحَابٞ مَّرۡكُومٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wain yaraw kisfan mina alssamai saqitan yaqooloo sahabun markoomun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if they were to see a fragment from the sky falling, they would say, "[It is merely] clouds heaped up."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: If they were to see a ˹deadly˺ piece of the sky fall down ˹upon them˺, still they would say, “˹This is just˺ a pile of clouds.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَذَرۡهُمۡ حَتَّىٰ يُلَٰقُواْ يَوۡمَهُمُ ٱلَّذِي فِيهِ يُصۡعَقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fatharhum hatta yulaqoo yawmahumu allathee feehi yusAAaqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So leave them until they meet their Day in which they will be struck insensible -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So leave them until they face their Day in which they will be struck dead—

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَ لَا يُغۡنِي عَنۡهُمۡ كَيۡدُهُمۡ شَيۡـٔٗا وَلَا هُمۡ يُنصَرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawma la yughnee AAanhum kayduhum shayan wala hum yunsaroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Day their plan will not avail them at all, nor will they be helped.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: the Day their scheming will be of no benefit to them whatsoever, nor will they be helped.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُواْ عَذَابٗا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكۡثَرَهُمۡ لَا يَعۡلَمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainna lillatheena thalamoo AAathaban doona thalika walakinna aktharahum la yaAAlamoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, for those who have wronged is a punishment before that, but most of them do not know.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Also, the wrongdoers will certainly have another torment before that ˹Day˺, but most of them do not know.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱصۡبِرۡ لِحُكۡمِ رَبِّكَ فَإِنَّكَ بِأَعۡيُنِنَاۖ وَسَبِّحۡ بِحَمۡدِ رَبِّكَ حِينَ تَقُومُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waisbir lihukmi rabbika fainnaka biaAAyunina wasabbih bihamdi rabbika heena taqoomu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And be patient, [O Muhammad], for the decision of your Lord, for indeed, you are in Our eyes. And exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord when you arise.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So be patient with your Lord’s decree, for you are truly under Our ˹watchful˺ Eyes. And glorify the praises of your Lord when you rise.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمِنَ ٱلَّيۡلِ فَسَبِّحۡهُ وَإِدۡبَٰرَ ٱلنُّجُومِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wamina allayli fasabbihhu waidbara alnnujoomi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And in a part of the night exalt Him and after [the setting of] the stars.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And glorify Him during part of the night and at the fading of the stars.
